contextProblemGoals.context: User problem: financial data analytics is fragmented (multiple sources), updates with delays, it's hard to spot anomalies/trends, and it's difficult to make fast decisions.
contextProblemGoals.goals: Provide a unified dashboard with near-real-time metrics, alerts, and interactive analytics (filters, drill-down), designed for large volumes of time series/transactions.

personas.name: Financial analyst
personas.description: Analytics-focused role consuming dashboards and reports.

personas.name: Risk manager/Compliance
personas.description: Monitors risk indicators and compliance metrics.

personas.name: Head of product/CEO
personas.description: High-level KPI overview for decision making.

scenarios.name: Deep slice
scenarios.description: Filter by time/geo/provider/currency; drill down to individual transactions.

nonFunctionalExpectations.description: Fast UI on large tables; predictable data refresh; explainability of metrics.

mustHave: Integration with 2-3 external APIs (e.g., FX rates, payments statistics, bank statements/provider)
ETL-lite layer: normalization, currency/timezone mapping, deduplication, storage of raw and normalized data
Dashboard: 6-10 key charts (Chart.js for standard charts, D3 for custom/complex), transaction table with server-side pagination/sorting/filtering

enhancements: Alerting: rules (thresholds, simplified anomaly detection), channels (email/Telegram/webhook)
Role-based access (RBAC) + action audit (who exported/viewed sensitive breakdowns)
Self-serve source configuration (UI for API keys, integration health checks, limits)

apiAndData.resilience: Rate limiting, retries with backoff, circuit breaker, idempotency on ingestion
apiAndData.observability: Tracing requests to external APIs, error/latency metrics per provider, data freshness report

cachingAndLoad.benefits: Caching reduces repeat API requests and speeds up responses; helps avoid provider limits

securityAndAuth.auth: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC, JWT access token + refresh token rotation; single-use refresh: rotate on refresh; old tokens invalidated

ciCdInfrastructure: CD (preview deployments on Vercel + prod on AWS)
tests: backend unit + contract tests for external APIs (mocks/fixtures)
frontend integration tests (Playwright/Cypress) for key scenarios

sections.notes: If stack is specified, provide tailored stories and acceptance criteria.
